successCriteria.description: src/modules/leave/leave.service.ts exists and exports ILeaveService interface and LeaveService class implementation
LeaveService validates all inputs using class-validator decorators before processing
LeaveService enforces business rules: start date must be >= current date, end date >= start date, total days > 0
LeaveService checks leave balance before creating requests and throws InsufficientBalanceError if balance insufficient
LeaveService writes audit records for all state-changing operations (create, update, delete) via IAuditService
LeaveService sends notifications for leave request submission and status changes via INotificationService
All state-changing operations execute in a single database transaction with audit logging and notifications as part of the same transaction
src/modules/leave/leave.service.spec.ts exists with Vitest unit tests covering validation logic, business rules, balance checks, audit logging, and notification sending

constraints: Repository pattern: All database access goes through repository interfaces
Audit records: All state-changing operations write an audit record
Input validation: Validate all inputs at API boundaries before processing
No sensitive data in logs: Never log passwords, tokens, PII, or financial data
RBAC enforcement: All API endpoints enforce role-based access control
Error handling: No unhandled promise rejections. All async errors are caught and handled
Dependency direction is leave -> employee, leave -> policy, leave -> balance, and leave -> notification
LeaveRequest lifecycle states are Pending, Approved, and Rejected
Balance deductions occur only during approveLeaveRequest
Notifications are created on submission, approval, and rejection events
Must implement ILeaveService interface with all methods defined
Must use class-validator decorators for input validation
Must use Vitest for unit testing (not Jest as mentioned in intent)
outOfScope: Controller layer with RBAC and error handling (Phase 6)

ambiguities.description: Intent mentions 'Jest unit tests' but project uses Vitest per architecture. Should tests be written with Vitest or Jest?
options: Use Vitest as per project architecture
Use Jest as mentioned in intent

ambiguities.description: Should the InsufficientBalanceError be defined in this phase or imported from another module?
options: Define new custom error class in leave module
Use generic Error with specific message

ambiguities.description: Should database transaction management be handled within LeaveService or delegated to repository?
